name: Yasomitra
dates_notes: Yaśomitra is normally placed in the sixth century from his position as a post-Vasubandhu commentator on the Abhidharmakośa tradition.
core_thesis: Yaśomitra is the author of the *Sphuṭārthā Abhidharmakośavyākhyā*, the major surviving Sanskrit commentary on Vasubandhu's *Abhidharmakośa* and *Kośabhāṣya*. His role is exegetical rather than sect-founding: he clarifies Vasubandhu's verses and prose, preserves technical Abhidharma definitions, and helps later readers distinguish the Vaibhāṣika positions presented in the verses from the Sautrāntika-leaning critiques often advanced in the bhāṣya. For this roster he supplies the main Sanskrit commentarial bridge between Vasubandhu's Abhidharma and later scholastic reception. A Wogihara-edition OCR has been added locally as a degraded source, but no doctrinal point is cited to exact loci here until that text is cleaned.

engaged_works.title: Sphutartha Abhidharmakosavyakhya
engaged_works.ascription_notes: The Sanskrit commentary is standardly ascribed to Yaśomitra and survives in printed editions, including Wogihara's 1932-1936 edition.
engaged_works.summary: A Sanskrit commentary on Vasubandhu's *Abhidharmakośa* and *Kośabhāṣya*. The work explains the technical vocabulary and doctrinal sequence of the Kośa chapters: dhātus, faculties, cosmology, karma, latent defilements, path, knowledge, meditative attainments, and the no-self argument. Its importance lies in its preservation of a Sanskrit scholastic reading of Vasubandhu, especially where the bhāṣya stages Vaibhāṣika and Sautrāntika alternatives. The local source is an Archive.org OCR of the Wogihara edition; it should be treated as degraded until corrected against page images.
